channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: EU-Tanzania Investment Forum 2026-2027 to Boost Economic Partnerships
item.description: The EU–Tanzania Investment and Business Forum 2026–2027 aims to strengthen economic ties between Tanzania and the European Union by fostering investment partnerships in key sectors like agriculture, e...

item.title: Tanzania prepares for Commonwealth Games with 12-member team
item.description: Tanzania has announced a 12-member team for the 2026 Commonwealth Games in Glasgow, Scotland. The squad includes six track and field athletes, three boxers, two judokas, and one swimmer. The team refl...

item.title: Oil Prices Surge Amid Escalating Tensions Between US and Iran Over Hormuz
item.description: Global oil prices surged over 4% on Monday as tensions between the United States and Iran escalated, with both nations launching military strikes that threatened the strategic Strait of Hormuz. Brent ...

item.title: Jovial Urges Women to Carefully Plan for Motherhood
item.description: Kenyan musician Jovial has advised women to carefully consider motherhood before having children, emphasizing the importance of emotional and financial readiness. In an interview with Alex Mwakideu, J...

item.title: Canadian Official Commends U.S. Senator for Advocacy of Democracy and Freedom
item.description: Prime Minister Justin Trudeau's former spokesperson, Chrystia Freeland, has praised U.S. Senator Lindsey Graham for his unwavering support of democratic values and individual freedoms during a recent ...

item.title: Jakarta's Waste Crisis: Struggles of Waste Pickers and Government Plans to Close the Landfill
item.description: Bantar Gebang, Jakarta's largest landfill, spans over 100 hectares and serves as both a dumping ground and a livelihood for thousands of waste pickers. The site, already over capacity, faces closure b...

item.title: Citizen Science Reveals Evolutionary Pathways of Parental Care in Harvestmen
item.description: A recent study highlights how citizen scientists, using platforms like iNaturalist, have contributed to understanding the evolutionary development of parental care in harvestmen (small arachnids). Res...

item.title: Ealdi reveals emotional breakdown at BST Hyde Park gig after Tourette's diagnosis uncertainty
item.description: During a recent performance at the BST Hyde Park festival, British singer Ealdi experienced an emotional breakdown on stage, openly expressing his vulnerability and uncertainty about continuing his mu...

item.title: Kaiser Reef reports 8% increase in gold production from Tassie and Vic mines
item.description: Kaiser Reef has achieved a 8.4% quarterly increase in gold production, reaching 6000 ounces across its Henty and Maldon operations. The Henty mine in Tasmania contributed 5563 ounces of gold and 5366 ...

item.title: Woman discovers her biological father through DNA testing after more than five decades of uncertainty
item.description: This first-person account by Debbie describes her lifelong search to discover the identity of her biological father, a journey that lasted more than 50 years and was ultimately resolved through modern...

item.title: Gum Disease Bacteria Linked to Heart Valve Damage Risk
item.description: A new study suggests that bacteria associated with gum disease, specifically Porphyromonas gingivalis, may contribute to calcific aortic valve stenosis (CAVS) by triggering inflammation and calcium bu...

item.title: Nigerian Stock Market Rebounds With Key Stocks Surging Over 15%
item.description: The Nigerian stock market experienced a strong rebound last week, with investors gaining over N9.3 trillion in just five days. The NGX market capitalisation rose to N156.444 trillion from N147.102 tri...

item.title: DOH Appoints Orthopedic Surgeon Jose Pujalte Jr. as New Head
item.description: The Department of Health (DOH) has announced the appointment of orthopedic surgeon Jose Pujalte Jr. as its new chief, effective July 14, 2026. Pujalte, a seasoned medical professional with over two de...

item.title: Security Forces Kill 5 Terrorists in Balochistan's Operation Shaban, Total Now 76
item.description: Security forces have killed another five terrorists during the ongoing Operation Shaban in Balochistan, bringing the total number of terrorists eliminated in the operation to 76. The operation, launch...

item.title: South Africa Seizes Three Million Rand in Drugs Along Mozambique Border
item.description: South African authorities have intercepted contraband worth a total of R9 million and stolen vehicles during June as part of Operation Corona. The majority of the seized drugs (R2.9 million) were reco...

item.title: Mumbai's Infrastructure Focus on Cars vs Public Transport Needs
item.description: Mumbai, a city primarily designed for commuters, is increasingly investing in road expansions for private vehicles despite the overwhelming reliance on public transport. The article highlights how bil...

item.title: Federal-Provincial Pipeline Deal Sparks Debate Over Trade Barriers and Economic Impact
item.description: The Canadian federal government has reached a deal with British Columbia (B.C.) and Alberta to construct a new crude oil pipeline from Alberta to the Pacific Coast, with B.C. receiving compensation fo...

item.title: Paula Prentiss: A 1960s Comedy Icon's Legacy
item.description: Paula Prentiss, an American actress renowned for her sharp wit, radiant smile, and comedic talent during the 1960s, emerged as a prominent figure in Hollywood. Born Paula Ragusa in 1938 in San Antonio...

item.title: South Africa's GDP Measurement Crisis and the R14.3-Trillion Structural Deficit
item.description: Pali Lehohla's article critiques South Africa's GDP measurement system, which incorrectly attributes economic output to Gauteng corporate headquarters rather than where value is actually created. This...

item.title: Country Thunder Sask. Fans Stay Resilient Despite Heatwave
item.description: The article highlights the resilience of Country Thunder Saskatchewan fans who continue to attend the annual music festival despite extreme heat conditions. While the event is described as 'nothing ma...
